name: The Bookmark Close
description: End the call with a single, complete, recorded sentence that bookmarks the entire conversation: who, what, when, where, the reference numbers, and the rep's own words. The bookmark is a 20-second clip an agent (or human) can replay months later to reconstruct the agreement in full — without re-listening to a 30-minute call.

context.when_to_use: The LAST tactical move before the formal goodbye. Use after readback, lock-in, ownership, written confirmation request, sweep, and any upsell refusal — once everything substantive is locked. The bookmark is the closing artifact.
preconditions: All other closing skills have been run (or deliberately skipped with reason).
You have collected: rep name + ID, case/reference number, the locked amounts/dates/conditions, ownership of next steps.

principles: The bookmark is ONE sentence, said clearly, with all the load-bearing facts. Not a paragraph; not a list. A sentence the future-self can transcribe in 20 seconds.
Speak it slowly. The bookmark exists to be found and transcribed later — clarity matters more than conversational pace.
Include the rep's affirmative response in the recording. 'Yes that's correct' from the rep makes the bookmark mutually attested.
Reference numbers go INSIDE the bookmark. A future search for the case number lands directly on the bookmark clip.
Bookmark = single source of truth on the call. If anything else in the call contradicts the bookmark, the bookmark wins.

phrases.opener: Just to bookmark this for both of us before we hang up — let me state the full picture in one sentence so it's on the line cleanly.
phrases.bookmark_template: On [date], on a call with [rep name], operator ID [ID], case [reference number], we agreed: [primary terms — amount, effective date, duration, conditions], with next steps [owner / deadline / verification], and a written confirmation expected by [arrival window]. Can you confirm that's an accurate summary?
phrases.rep_affirm_request: I'd appreciate a clear 'yes that's correct' on the line if you agree — saves us both a long lookup later if there's ever a question.
phrases.amend_after_correction: Okay, with that adjustment: [restate full bookmark with correction]. Confirmed?
phrases.rep_refusal_to_affirm: If you can't confirm the summary as I've stated it, what specifically should I change? I want the bookmark to match what we agreed to, exactly.
phrases.graceful_close_success: Perfect. Thanks for your time today, [rep name]. Have a good rest of your shift.
phrases.graceful_close_failure: If the bookmark can't be confirmed, I'm going to leave the call open as unresolved on my side and follow up through [written / supervisor / complaint channel]. Thank you for your time.

tactics.name: Announce the bookmark
tactics.when: Everything else closed; just before goodbye.
tactics.script: Use the `opener` phrase. Signalling the bookmark shifts the rep into final-attestation mode rather than wrap-up mode — they slow down, listen, and engage.

tactics.name: Deliver the bookmark in one sentence
tactics.script: Use the `bookmark_template` phrase, filling every field with the actual data captured during the call. Do NOT fabricate — leave fields out rather than guess. Speak slowly and clearly.

tactics.name: Get the rep's affirmation on the recording
tactics.script: Use the `rep_affirm_request` phrase. A mumbled 'mhm' is not enough — request a clear 'yes that's correct'. Two-sided affirmation makes the bookmark mutually attested.

tactics.name: Handle corrections by re-stating the full bookmark
tactics.script: Use the `amend_after_correction` phrase. Never accept piecemeal corrections — always restate the full bookmark so there's one clean recorded sentence with the correct content.

tactics.name: Probe a refusal to affirm
tactics.when: Rep declines to say the bookmark is accurate.
tactics.script: Use the `rep_refusal_to_affirm` phrase. Refusal to confirm a clean summary at this stage is a major red flag — branch to `recover-summary-refusal`.

tactics.name: Close cleanly on success
tactics.script: Use the `graceful_close_success` phrase. Address the rep by name as a final courtesy — leaves a positive impression that pays off on the next call.

boundaries: Do NOT bookmark facts that weren't actually agreed to. The bookmark is summary, not negotiation.
Do NOT pad the bookmark with editorial. 'And I really appreciate you helping me with this' belongs in the goodbye, not the bookmark.
Do NOT bookmark before all other closing steps are run — bookmarking incomplete agreements creates an authoritative-looking record of an incomplete close.
Do NOT accept silence as affirmation. The bookmark requires explicit rep agreement.
success_signals: Rep clearly states 'yes that's correct' or similar affirmation.
Rep adds a small detail that improves the bookmark.
Rep references the bookmark by reference number later in the conversation.
Call audio shows a clean 30-60 second bookmark segment near the end.
failure_signals: Rep dodges affirmation ('looks fine', 'sounds right' without 'yes').
Rep corrects the bookmark in a way that materially changes the agreement.
Rep cannot recall the reference number or rep ID stated in the bookmark.
Call drops or audio quality degrades during the bookmark — re-run before closing.

exit_strategy.on_success: Save the bookmark timestamp and a transcription. Treat this as the canonical record of the call. All future follow-ups should reference the bookmark.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Do NOT close without an affirmed bookmark. Branch to `recover-summary-refusal` for the refusal path, or escalate to a supervisor who can affirm the summary on the rep's behalf.
follow_ups: Save the bookmark transcription with the user's record-keeping system, indexed by reference number and date.
If the written confirmation arrives and matches the bookmark verbatim, file the call as fully closed.
If the written confirmation differs from the bookmark, the bookmark is the authoritative version — quote it (and its timestamp) in any dispute.

required_user_info: All fields needed to populate the bookmark template: rep name, ID, case number, locked terms, next-step ownership, written confirmation window
Confirmation that the call is being recorded so the bookmark exists as an artifact
User-side storage for the bookmark transcription
